@settings.network.permissionWithout.description: Explains what still works without the Local Network permission. "Cmdr" is the product name; keep it. "mounted" = a share already attached to the system; "shares" = network folders.

@settings.network.customTimeoutUnit.description: Unit suffix shown after the custom-timeout number input (the value is in seconds).

@settings.updates.emailPlaceholder.description: Placeholder text in the beta-contact email field. "you@example.com" is a literal example email address; keep it exactly.

@settings.updates.checkForUpdates.description: Button that manually triggers an update check. In Settings > Updates & privacy.

@settings.updates.errorPrefix.description: Label preceding an update-check error message. Followed by the specific message. Keep the trailing colon. (Developer/diagnostic context, so the word "Error" is acceptable here.)

@settings.updates.sendErrorReport.description: Button that sends an error report after an update check fails. In Settings > Updates & privacy.

settings.updates.emailConfirmHint: Check your inbox to confirm your email. Thanks for helping out!

@settings.updates.emailConfirmHint.description: Friendly confirmation shown after the user submits their beta email, asking them to confirm via the inbox link. Warm, appreciative voice.

settings.updates.emailSignupError: Sorry, we couldn''t sign you up right now. Try again?

@settings.updates.emailSignupError.description: Apologetic message shown when the beta-email signup fails. Conversational and reassuring; do not use the words "error" or "failed".

settings.updates.emailPrivacyNote: Stored only on your Mac. We never send it together with your anonymous usage data, so your stats can''t be tied back to you. Used only to reach out and to optionally attach to a report you send. To stop getting emails, use the unsubscribe link in any message we send.

@settings.updates.emailPrivacyNote.description: Privacy note under the beta-email field, reassuring the user about how the email is stored and used. "Mac" is the Apple computer brand name; keep it. "stats" = statistics.

@settings.logging.openLogFile.description: Button that opens the app's log file. In Settings > Developer > Logging.

@settings.logging.copied.description: Brief confirmation shown after diagnostic info is copied to the clipboard. Pairs with "Copy diagnostic info". Keep the exclamation if it suits the locale.

@settings.logging.copyDiagnostics.description: Button that copies diagnostic information to the clipboard. Briefly becomes "Copied!" after use.

@settings.mcp.checkPort.description: Button that tests whether the configured MCP server port is available. "port" is a networking term. In Settings > Developer > MCP server.

@settings.mcp.runningOnPort.description: Status line shown when the MCP server is active, reporting the actual bound port. "port" is a networking term.

placeholders.runningPort: the actual network port number the server bound to, e.g. "52341"

@settings.mcp.ephemeral.description: Suffix appended after the running port to mark it as an OS-picked temporary port. "ephemeral" is a networking term for an auto-assigned short-lived port. Keep the parentheses.

@settings.mcp.portInUseFallback.description: Suffix explaining the preferred port was taken, so the server fell back to another. "port" is a networking term. Keep the parentheses.

placeholders.port: the preferred port number the user requested that was unavailable, e.g. "8080"

@settings.mcp.checkingPort.description: Status shown while testing whether the MCP port is free. The trailing ellipsis signals an in-progress action; keep it.

@settings.mcp.portAvailable.description: Status confirming the chosen MCP port is free to use. "Port" is a networking term.

placeholders.port: the port number that was checked, e.g. "8080"

settings.mcp.portInUseStillRunning: Port {port} is in use, still running on port {runningPort}

@settings.mcp.portInUseStillRunning.description: Status shown when the preferred MCP port is taken but the server is already running on a different port. "Port" is a networking term.

placeholders.port: the preferred port number that is unavailable, e.g. "8080"
placeholders.runningPort: the port the server is actually running on, e.g. "52341"

@settings.mcp.portInUse.description: Status warning that the chosen MCP port is already taken. "Port" is a networking term.

placeholders.port: the port number that is unavailable, e.g. "8080"

@settings.mcp.usePortInstead.description: Button/link suggesting an alternative free MCP port when the chosen one is taken. "port" is a networking term.

placeholders.suggestedPort: the alternative free port number being suggested, e.g. "52000"

settings.advanced.warningBanner: These settings are for advanced users. Incorrect values may cause performance issues or unexpected behavior.

@settings.advanced.warningBanner.description: Cautionary banner at the top of the Advanced settings section, warning that changing these may degrade performance or stability.

@settings.advanced.resetAll.description: Button that resets every advanced setting to its default value. In the Advanced section.

settings.advanced.resetAllConfirm: Reset all advanced settings to their defaults? This cannot be undone.

@settings.advanced.resetAllConfirm.description: Confirmation-dialog body shown before resetting all advanced settings. Warns the action is irreversible.

@settings.advanced.resetAllConfirmTitle.description: Title of the confirmation dialog for resetting all advanced settings. Keep concise.

@settings.advanced.defaultPrefix.description: Label preceding a setting's default value in the Advanced section. Followed by the value. Keep the trailing colon.

settings.applier.logStorageRestart: Restart Cmdr to apply the log storage change.

@settings.applier.logStorageRestart.description: Toast shown after the log-storage setting changes, telling the user a restart is needed for it to take effect. "Cmdr" is the product name; keep it.
